description: Domain vocabulary for the FastForex currency exchange rate API

terms.description: The source/base currency symbol used in a rate query or conversion
Single exchange rate result keyed by target currency symbol
Multiple exchange rate results keyed by target currency symbol
Timestamp when the exchange rate data was last updated
Server-side response processing time in milliseconds
An FX trading pair in XXXYYY or XXX/YYY format (e.g. EURUSD or EUR/USD)
A collection of trading pairs with metadata
The bid (buy) price for an FX trading pair
The ask (sell) price for an FX trading pair
Unix timestamp in milliseconds for the quote price
Quote size in units of the base currency (usually 1, but may be larger for minor currencies)
ISO8601 duration interval for time-series data (e.g. P1D=daily, PT1H=hourly, PT1M=minutely)
Desired datetime format for responses: ISO (ISO8601), TSP (Unix millisecond timestamp), UTCYMD (UTC date+time string)
Timezone offset applied to datetime values in the response
OHLC candle open price
OHLC candle high price
OHLC candle low price
OHLC candle close price
Datetime label for a time-series data point
Amount of source currency being converted
Exchange rate used in a currency conversion
Number of API calls consumed by a request (some endpoints charge per pair returned)
Map of currency symbols to their full names
Total API call quota for the current billing month
Current billing period details including start, end, usage, and remaining quota
Number of API calls remaining in the current billing period
Historical API usage data keyed by date
Alternative trading pair identifier (e.g. EURUSD as alternate form of EUR/USD)
Price type selector: bid or ask
Two-dimensional map of from/to currency exchange rates
